title: Reported Speech — Questions
description: Reporting questions drops the inverted word order and uses IF / WHETHER for yes/no questions.
summary_pt_br: Perguntas reportadas: sem inversão e com IF/WHETHER. 'Do you work?' → She asked if I worked.

scenes.subtitle_en: Reporting questions drops the inverted word order and uses IF / WHETHER for yes/no questions.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Perguntas reportadas: sem inversão e com IF/WHETHER. 'Do you work?' → She asked if I worked.

scenes.text_en: Reporting questions drops the inverted word order and uses IF / WHETHER for yes/no questions. Today's focus: reported speech — questions. She asked if I worked at the school.
scenes.text_pt: Perguntas reportadas: sem inversão e com IF/WHETHER. 'Do you work?' → She asked if I worked. Foco de hoje: reported speech — questions. Ela perguntou se eu trabalhava na escola.

scenes.title: Reported Speech — Questions
scenes.body_en: 'Do you work here?' → She asked IF I worked there. Wh-questions keep the question word: 'Where do you live?' → He asked where I lived. No inversion, no question mark.
scenes.body_pt: Perguntas reportadas: sem inversão e com IF/WHETHER. 'Do you work?' → She asked if I worked.

examples.en: She asked if I worked at the school.
examples.pt: Ela perguntou se eu trabalhava na escola.

examples.en: He wanted to know where I lived.
examples.pt: Ele queria saber onde eu morava.

examples.en: They asked whether the meeting had started.
examples.pt: Eles perguntaram se a reunião tinha começado.

exercise.question: Complete: "She asked if I ___ at the school."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "She asked if I worked at the school."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Ela perguntou se eu trabalhava na escola.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: He wanted to know where I lived.
I haven't heard anything about that.
exercise.explanation: The correct sentence is: "He wanted to know where I lived."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Ele queria saber onde eu morava.

exercise.explanation: Five key terms from the lesson. Match each English word to its Portuguese meaning.

scenes.title: Reported Speech — Questions — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre reported speech — questions. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: The recruiter asked me a lot of questions in yesterday's interview.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: She asked where I had studied computer science.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Did she ask about your previous job?

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Yes. She wanted to know why I had left the last company.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: I said I wanted more responsibility and bigger challenges.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Did she ask when you could start?

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: She asked whether I could start within two weeks.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: I told her I could start next Monday if needed.

scenes.title: The job interview debrief
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, detalhes e sua opinião. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Write the questions the recruiter asked and how Joca answered them. Use at least three reported questions.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Resumo curto com quem falou, o que disseram e sua opinião.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: After the interview, Joca met his friend Zara at the café to talk about how it went.

paragraphs.speaker: Zara
paragraphs.en: So, how did the interview go? What did she ask you?

paragraphs.speaker: Joca
paragraphs.en: She asked where I had studied and why I had left my last job.

paragraphs.speaker: Recruiter
paragraphs.en: Could you tell me about a time when you solved a difficult problem?

paragraphs.speaker: Joca
paragraphs.en: I told her about the time I fixed a production bug during a holiday.

paragraphs.speaker: Zara
paragraphs.en: That sounds great. Did she mention when you would hear back?

paragraphs.speaker: Joca
paragraphs.en: She said the team would call me within five business days.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Reported Speech — Questions". Reporting questions drops the inverted word order and uses IF / WHETHER for yes/no questions. She asked if I worked at the school. He wanted to know where I lived. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: Prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Reported Speech — Questions". I liked it because it was clear and useful. See you next time!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule from last year
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala o título: "Reported Speech — Questions".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para continuar praticando. Salve os links e volte sempre.
